steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage establishes a core problem: when multiple substrates share similar features, it becomes impossible to determine which one contributed a given word or structure to the creole. The correct choice must illustrate this attribution problem.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A: attribution is confirmed — resolves the problem rather than illustrating it. ✗
  • Choice B: a feature is already credited to a source despite overlapping origins — doesn't show the problem of being unable to attribute. ✗
  • Choice C: addresses speech variation unrelated to substrate origin — off-topic. ✗
  • Choice D: Hawaiian Pidgin's verb "ste" has comparable usage in three substrates (Ōlelo Hawai'i, Portuguese, and Spanish), making definitive attribution impossible — directly illustrates the passage's core problem. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
